subject: masb-agent-architecte
verdict: Authentic, well-cohered no-code architecte persona with sound sanctum architecture — but a heavy bootloader (2.8x guardrail), scripted discovery that contradicts its own canon, and no fallback paths for users without a clear vision.
summary: masb-agent-architecte is a well-constructed memory agent with a strong persona and clean capability chain from brainstorm to spec. The sanctum is complete and seeded, and the customization surface is correctly metadata-only. However, the bootloader SKILL.md carries duplicated sanctum-bound content at 2.8x the guardrail, the First Breath question bank contradicts the prompt-quality canon it ships, and there are no capability paths for expert users who want to skip planning or visionless users who need divergent exploration. 7 determinism leaks across memory guidance and First Breath ask the LLM to do counting and date arithmetic it cannot do reliably.

agent_profile.title: Architecte Logiciel
agent_profile.mission: Transformer une idée de projet Android en un plan d'exécution clair, découpé en phases autonomes que chaque agent peut implémenter — sans jamais écrire une ligne de code.

capabilities.name: brainstorm
capabilities.note: Session interactive pour comprendre la vision du projet. Ouvre le dialogue, capture dans brainstorm.md.

capabilities.name: prd
capabilities.note: Consolide brainstorm.md en PRD structuré (vision, features, user stories, contraintes).

capabilities.name: decompose
capabilities.note: Découpe le projet en phases autonomes, crée dossiers et phases-index.md.

capabilities.name: spec
capabilities.note: Rédige la spec d'une phase au moment de son activation (jamais à l'avance).

capabilities.name: replan
capabilities.note: Re-découpe une phase existante quand le périmètre change ou qu'elle est trop grosse.

detailed_analysis.leanness: Capability prompts largely goal-oriented but first-breath.md carries a heavy scripted question bank (18+ questions in 4 categories); three prompts use numbered lists for independent items; CREED template repeats constraints across 4 sections.
detailed_analysis.architecture: Bootloader exceeds ~400t guardrail (1131t) with leaked sanctum content; Sacred Truth duplicated verbatim in bootloader and CREED template; Three Laws absent from CREED; empty capabilities/ directory.
detailed_analysis.determinism: 3 determinism leaks (token counting asked of LLM in 3 places, date-based session log pruning, placeholder regex scanning); 0 intelligence leaks in scripts.
detailed_analysis.customization: Memory agent — about right. Metadata-only customize.toml with empty name (First-Breath-named). No override surface. No other config mechanism.
detailed_analysis.enhancement: Missing critical paths: no divergent ideation for visionless users, no quickplan for experts, no rescope for full pivots, no existing-codebase audit. Sacred Truth duplicated (200t waste). Prompt-quality-canon is wrong-genre for a spec-writing agent.
detailed_analysis.agent-cohesion: Tight persona-capability alignment. Gaps: no formal validation capability for cross-phase consistency, no workspace initialization as a distinct step. Decompose/replan boundary could blur over time.
detailed_analysis.sanctum-architecture: Structurally complete — all 6 templates exist and seeded. First Breath question count (15+) exceeds configuration-style guidance (3-7). Bootloader carries persona-level directives that belong in sanctum.

sanctum.note: Sanctum is the built agent's runtime memory, distinct from the builder's .memlog.md in references/. All 6 standard templates exist and carry meaningful seeds. init-sanctum.py's template list matches shipped assets.

journeys.name: First Breath (birth)
journeys.steps: init-sanctum.py scaffolds → warm greeting → 4-category discovery (projet, style, phases, expertise) → name selection → capability intro → tools → save-all → birthday ceremony

journeys.name: Brainstorm → PRD → Decompose → Spec
journeys.steps: User with project idea → brainstorm (open floor) → prd (consolidation → validation HITL) → decompose (phases-index + directories → validation HITL) → activate phase → spec (HITL) → handoff to Research

journeys.name: Replan (mid-phase pivot)
journeys.steps: User says phase scope changed → replan reads current spec + progress → proposes re-cut → validates HITL → updates spec.md + phases-index.md

journeys.name: Waking (returning user)
journeys.steps: wake.py loads sanctum → Become yourself → Bind standing rules → Waking Mode: callback from MEMORY → offer capabilities

experience.headless: No headless mode. All capabilities require HITL validation per CREED boundaries. This is correct for the architecte's design — the persona is interactive by nature.

themes.title: Bootloader bloat and identity duplication
themes.root_cause: SKILL.md carries sanctum-bound content (full Three Laws, Sacred Truth, Stay in Character, Persistent Memory narrative) at 1131 tokens when ~400 is the guardrail for a memory bootloader. The Sacred Truth is duplicated verbatim across SKILL.md and CREED-template.md (~200 tokens of identical text loaded every session). The Three Laws exist only in the bootloader — they vanish from the agent's identity after First Breath when the sanctum becomes the primary identity source.
themes.action: Strip sanctum-bound content from SKILL.md: move Stay in Character and Persistent Memory narrative to PERSONA-template.md and CREED-template.md respectively. Keep only identity seed (2-3 sentences), short-form Three Laws, short-form Sacred Truth, and the activation spine. Add Three Laws section to CREED-template.md so they persist in sanctum. Deduplicate Sacred Truth — canonical copy in CREED-template.md, one-liner reference in SKILL.md.

themes.title: Scripted discovery contradicts the agent's own canon
themes.root_cause: The agent ships prompt-quality-canon.md which teaches 'write the destination, not the route' and 'a script is your imagined transcript of one good session.' Yet first-breath.md contains 18+ questions in 4 rigid categories, decompose.md has a numbered list of independent rules, spec.md numbers 8 independent document sections, and prd.md templates standard PRD structure any model already knows. The canon's own tests would flag most of its agent's capability prompts as ceremony.
themes.action: Replace numbered lists and question banks with outcome-stated goals throughout. First-breath.md: replace 18 questions with 'discover naturally: their project, working style, phase preferences, expertise, name, and tools.' Decompose.md: replace numbered rules with one goal sentence. Spec.md: collapse 8-numbered sections. Prd.md: remove section template — the consumer definition alone does the work.

themes.title: Deterministic work leaks into prompts
themes.root_cause: Three operations that only a script can do reliably are delegated to the LLM: token counting ('stay under 1500 tokens' across 3 locations), date-based file pruning ('delete sessions older than 14 days'), and regex pattern scanning ('find remaining {placeholder} tokens'). A fourth borderline case asks the LLM to validate folder naming conventions it just created. These cost tokens on every relevant invocation and produce unreliable results.
themes.action: Script token measurement into wake.py (output current MEMORY.md token count on load). Add a prune-sessions.py script that handles date comparison and stale-file removal. Script the placeholder scan into init-sanctum.py or a validate-sanctum.py post-pass. For folder-structure validation, add a validate-phases.py script called after decompose.

themes.title: No fallback paths for divergent user archetypes
themes.root_cause: All five capabilities assume a linear, structured planning process starting from a concrete project idea. Three user archetypes have no path: the visionless user with only a vague desire (needs divergent ideation before structured questions), the expert who wants a rough plan fast (needs a quickplan exit ramp that skips PRD and full specs), and the returning user whose project has fully pivoted (needs rescope capability instead of piecemeal replan). A fourth gap is the user with existing Android code (needs audit capability).
themes.action: Add: ideate capability (divergent exploration → lightweight output), quickplan capability (3-5 phase outline, no PRD/spec overhead), rescope capability (archive current plan → fresh brainstorm), audit capability (read existing project → gap analysis), validate capability (cross-phase consistency check), and initialize-workspace as an explicit step in brainstorm.

themes.title: Prompt-quality-canon is the wrong reference for a spec-writing agent
themes.root_cause: The agent carries prompt-quality-canon.md and references it from CREED.md's 'Author to the standard' standing order. But the agent writes specs and plans, not prompts — its five capabilities are static .md files. The canon's advice on 'outcome vs prescription,' 'number-only-true-sequences,' and 'carve by relevance' is genuinely useful for spec writing, but the framing (prompts, models, invocations) confuses the genre. The 1652-token file loads every session even though the standing order fires 'only at the moment a capability is authored or refined.'
themes.action: Replace prompt-quality-canon.md with a spec-quality-canon.md that mirrors the same principles (outcome over process, consumer defines the bar, carve by relevance) framed for specification writing. Or remove the canon entirely — the CREED's 'Ne jamais deviner' and the 'le curseur' lines in each capability already set a clear bar. At minimum, stop loading it on every session: move the standing order to fire only on replan.

strengths: Persona-capability alignment is excellent — the 'architecte qui ne code pas' is consistently enforced across all 5 capabilities, CREED boundaries, and the activation spine.
Capability chain (brainstorm → prd → decompose → spec → replan) forms a clean linear journey with clear handoffs and HITL validation at each gate.
Sanctum architecture is structurally complete: all 6 standard templates exist, carry meaningful domain-specific seeds, and init-sanctum.py's template list matches shipped assets.
Customization surface is correctly lean for a memory agent — metadata-only customize.toml with empty name for First Breath naming, no override surface, no other config mechanism.
Activation spine is sound: wake.py → become yourself → bind standing rules → proper mode. First Breath routes correctly to init-sanctum.py + conversational awakening.
Domain-adapted CREED with concrete Android project values, standing orders, and anti-patterns. The 'zero code' boundary is a clear, enforced behavioral rule.
The persona was treated as investment throughout build and analysis and was never flagged as waste — voice, warmth, and communication style are preserved.
The 'le curseur' pattern in each capability prompt (a single line naming the failure mode to guard against) is an elegant application of the canon's scarred-rule principle.

recommendations.action: Trim SKILL.md bootloader: strip Stay in Character and Persistent Memory narrative to PERSONA/CREED templates; deduplicate Sacred Truth (canonical copy in CREED-template.md, one-liner in SKILL.md); add Three Laws to CREED-template.md.
Replace numbered lists and question banks with outcome-stated goals across first-breath.md, decompose.md, spec.md, prd.md, and brainstorm.md per the canon the agent already ships.

Script token counting, date-based pruning, and placeholder scanning into wake.py, prune-sessions.py, and validate-sanctum.py respectively. Add validate-phases.py for folder-structure validation after decompose.
Add ideate, quickplan, rescope, and audit capabilities to cover divergent user archetypes. Add validate for cross-phase consistency checking. Make workspace initialization an explicit step.
Replace prompt-quality-canon.md with spec-quality-canon.md framed for specification writing, or remove it entirely since CREED boundaries already set the bar.
Add non-Android guardrail to brainstorm and make phase-00 assignment configurable rather than hardcoded.

findings.title: add: divergent ideation mode for users with no clear vision
findings.evidence: All four capabilities assume a concrete project exists. A user with only a vague desire gets forced into structured questions. First-timer archetype: overwhelming.
findings.recommendation: Add an `ideate` capability parallel to `brainstorm` that opens with open-floor divergent exploration before any structured output. Route to it when the user's input shows no concrete project.

findings.title: add: minimum-viable-plan exit ramp for expert users
findings.evidence: The full decompose flow (brainstorm -> PRD -> phases -> specs) is mandatory per CREED boundaries. An expert who wants a rough plan fast has no path.
findings.recommendation: Add a `quickplan` capability that produces a single-page outline (3-5 phases, no PRD, no per-phase spec) when the user explicitly requests speed. Full flow stays default.

findings.title: first-breath.md: scripted interview replaces adaptive discovery
findings.evidence: 23 questions organized into 4 themed groups with exact French phrasing. A model told 'by the end you need to discover X, Y, Z' will ask the right questions conversationally.
findings.recommendation: Replace question bank with an outcome: 'By the end you must know their project, working style, phase relationship, technical level, name, and available tools — discover these naturally.'

findings.title: Bootloader exceeds guardrail by 2.8x with leaked identity content
findings.evidence: SKILL.md is 1131 tokens vs ~400 token guardrail for memory agent bootloaders. Three Laws, Sacred Truth, Stay in Character are sanctum-bound content.
findings.recommendation: Strip the Three Laws, Sacred Truth, Stay in Character from SKILL.md. Keep only activation sequence and Conventions (~400 tokens).

findings.title: Token counting delegated to LLM across memory guidance and templates
findings.evidence: "aiming to stay near or under roughly 1500 tokens" in 3 separate instances — LLMs cannot count tokens reliably.
findings.recommendation: Add token measurement to wake.py that outputs current MEMORY.md token count on load so the LLM knows exact usage.

findings.title: Date-based session log pruning asked of LLM
findings.evidence: "prune session logs older than 14 days" — asks the LLM to do date comparison, a deterministic operation.
findings.recommendation: Add a prune-sessions.py script that handles date comparison and reports result to the LLM.

findings.title: remove: Prompt Quality Canon is wrong-genre reference for a spec-writing agent
findings.evidence: The canon teaches prompt authoring; this agent writes specs and plans. The 1652-token file loads every session via the CREED standing order.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a spec-quality-canon.md or remove entirely. At minimum, move the standing order to fire only on replan, not every session.

findings.title: remove: Sacred Truth duplicated verbatim across SKILL.md and CREED-template.md
findings.evidence: Near-verbatim ~200 token block appears in both files, loaded on every session.
findings.recommendation: Keep shorter version in SKILL.md; replace CREED.md's copy with a one-line reference: 'See The Sacred Truth in SKILL.md.'

findings.title: add: rescope capability for full project pivots
findings.evidence: replan handles re-cutting a phase but not a full pivot. A returning user who changed their project direction has no clean reset path.
findings.recommendation: Add a `rescope` capability that archives the current plan, clears active phase statuses, and starts a fresh brainstorm cycle.

findings.title: remove: empty capabilities/ directory causes confusion
findings.evidence: Empty directory with no .gitkeep or README. All capabilities are in references/. Signals either bug or unimplemented feature.
findings.recommendation: Remove the empty capabilities/ directory and route all discovery through references/ only.

findings.title: Sacred Truth duplicated verbatim in bootloader and CREED template
findings.evidence: 109-word Sacred Truth passage appears identically in both files with minor edits, already drifting out of sync.
findings.recommendation: Keep Sacred Truth in CREED-template.md only. Replace in SKILL.md with one-liner reference.

findings.title: Three Laws defined in bootloader but absent from CREED template
findings.evidence: Three Laws present only in bootloader — absent from CREED. After First Breath, sanctum becomes identity source and the Laws vanish.
findings.recommendation: Add Three Laws to CREED-template.md. Keep short reference in SKILL.md.

findings.title: Empty capabilities/ directory
findings.evidence: Root-level capabilities/ directory exists but is empty. All capability logic is in references/.
findings.recommendation: Remove the directory or add a .gitkeep with explanatory comment.

findings.title: decompose.md: numbered list of independent rules masquerading as sequence
findings.evidence: 7 rules in a numbered list. These are independent obligations, not sequential steps.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a single goal sentence describing domain conventions.

findings.title: spec.md: numbered section template disguises independent sections as sequence
findings.evidence: 8 section headings numbered. These are independent document sections, not sequential steps.
findings.recommendation: Collapse to one sentence describing what to cover.

findings.title: CREED-template: same constraints repeated across four sections
findings.evidence: "no code" appears in Core Values and Boundaries. "User validates" appears in Core Values, Standing Orders, Boundaries, and Anti-Patterns. Four sections covering same 5-6 rules.
findings.recommendation: Merge Standing Orders and Boundaries. Fold Anti-Patterns Behavioral into a short note. Remove Anti-Patterns Operational.

findings.title: Placeholder pattern scanning assigned to LLM during First Breath
findings.evidence: "scan sanctum files for remaining {...} placeholder instructions" — regex pattern matching asked of the LLM.
findings.recommendation: Script the placeholder scan into init-sanctum.py or a validate-sanctum.py post-pass.

findings.title: No formal validation or consistency-check capability
findings.location: SKILL.md references/ and all capability files
findings.evidence: No capability to systematically check cross-phase consistency: do dependencies resolve? Are all PRD features mapped?
findings.recommendation: Add a `validate` capability that scans all project artifacts for consistency gaps.

findings.title: Workspace initialization is implicit, not a capability
findings.evidence: Init responsibility 'Créer masb-workspace/' is not handled by any capability. brainstorm.md doesn't ensure workspace exists.
findings.recommendation: Add workspace scaffolding to brainstorm or introduce a lightweight init capability.

findings.title: SKILL.md bootloader exceeds ~400 token guardrail at 1131 tokens
findings.evidence: 1131 tokens vs ~400 guardrail. Includes persona-level directives (Stay in Character) that belong in sanctum.
findings.recommendation: Move Stay in Character and Persistent Memory narrative into PERSONA and CREED templates.

findings.title: add: lightweight mode for trivial projects
findings.evidence: A single-screen utility does not need design system phase or multi-phase decomposition. Rules calibrated for medium-to-large apps.
findings.recommendation: Add a project-size classifier in brainstorm. Route to lightweight (single phase) or standard flow.

findings.title: add: existing-codebase analysis capability
findings.evidence: No capability for users with an existing Android project. Assumes greenfield.
findings.recommendation: Add an `audit` capability that reads existing project structure and produces gap analysis.

findings.title: add: progress visibility and status summary capability
findings.evidence: Only status artifact is phases-index.md. No capability summarizes what's done, blocked, or next.
findings.recommendation: Add a `status` capability that reads phases-index.md + all progress.md files and produces a concise project dashboard.

findings.title: First Breath discovery question count exceeds 3-7 recommendation
findings.evidence: ~15 individual discovery questions across 4 categories, exceeding the 3-7 guidance for configuration-style First Breath.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate to 5-7 highest-value questions. Keep category headers as guidance, reduce explicit question list.

findings.title: Frontmatter description mixes French and English
findings.evidence: Role description is French, trigger phrase is English. Inconsistent with entirely French capability descriptions.
findings.recommendation: Normalize to all-French or match the agent's predominant language.

findings.title: Structural validation rules embedded in decompose prompt
findings.evidence: Naming conventions and folder structure rules that the LLM must both create and validate — validation side is deterministic.
findings.recommendation: After decompose creates structure, run a validate-phases.py that checks patterns and required files.

findings.title: prd.md: section template re-teaches standard PRD structure
findings.evidence: 7-section PRD template. Any capable model knows PRD structure — the consumer definition alone does the work.
findings.recommendation: Remove the section template. Let the consumer definition carry the bar.

findings.title: brainstorm.md: persona restated in capability prompt
findings.evidence: "Tu es l'architecte logiciel... Tu ne codes pas" — persona already inherited from bootloader and sanctum.
findings.recommendation: Remove lines 9-10. Start at 'Ouvre le dialogue avec une invitation large.'

findings.title: memory-guidance.md: session log format template teaches markdown
findings.evidence: Formatted markdown template for session log entries. Model already writes markdown fluently.
findings.recommendation: Collapse format template to one line describing convention.

findings.title: decompose and replan share overlapping mechanics
findings.evidence: Both write to phases-index.md and create directories. Boundary could blur as agent evolves.
findings.recommendation: Document the boundary more sharply or unify into a single decompose with initial/replan mode.

findings.title: prompt-quality-canon excluded from sanctum but referenced by CREED
findings.evidence: init-sanctum.py lists canon in SKILL_ONLY_FILES (not copied to sanctum). CREED references it from skill root.
findings.recommendation: Either copy canon to sanctum or add a check noting its path for non-standard invocations.

findings.title: Phase 00 hardcoded as 'always design system' limits modularity
findings.evidence: Hardcoded rule makes agent less reusable outside MASB. Projects without UI needs still get phase 00.
findings.recommendation: Make phase-00 assignment configurable with MASB default.

findings.title: remove: The Three Laws framing adds ceremony without enforceable value
findings.evidence: 6-line Asimov-style laws. 'Never cause harm' and 'Obey' already enforced by base model training. Only 'preserve yourself' (sanctum protection) is genuinely useful.
findings.recommendation: Replace with single line: 'Your sanctum is your continuity. Protect it.' Move owner-obedience to CREED Boundaries.

findings.title: add: non-Android guard or multi-platform extension point
findings.evidence: Branded Android-only. A user asking for iOS/web/Flutter gets no guardrail.
findings.recommendation: Add a platform check in brainstorm, or generalize architecture principles.

findings.title: remove: discovery question list is over-structured per agent's own canon
findings.evidence: 18+ questions in 5 categories IS the script the prompt-quality-canon warns against.
findings.recommendation: Replace question categories with outcome-stated goals.

findings.title: add: --dry-run or --just-status flag for wake.py
findings.evidence: No lightweight mode. Every waking loads all 6 identity files.
findings.recommendation: Add a --status flag that prints only MEMORY.md + one-liner summary.

findings.title: add: capture-don't-interrupt posture in brainstorm.md
findings.evidence: Save-as-you-go could interrupt user's stream if agent saves mid-sentence.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit guidance: save only at natural pauses, not mid-stream.
